summary: Near‑perfect Miller soundboard mix captures a spectacular 1972 performance, with a monster “The Other One” and a scorching “He’s Gone” leading the way.
review: Reviewers rave about this tape as a Miller masterpiece – the mix is crystal clear with virtually invisible patchwork, letting the band’s expansive jams shine. The show is highlighted by an awe‑inspiring Phil bass stomp, a legendary run of “The Other One,” and a fiery ending to “He’s Gone,” making it one of the finest fall‑’72 outings.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviews are overwhelmingly positive (four 5‑star, one 4‑star) and specifically praise both the musical performance and the superb Miller soundboard mix.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Miller’s masterful mix is described as superb, near‑perfect, with only barely noticeable patches; clarity and balance are repeatedly praised.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert is lauded for its expansive, flowing jams and a powerful Phil‑bass “stomp,” delivering a cohesive, high‑energy experience from start to finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mix balances his guitar beautifully; reviewers note the tone as part of the superb overall sound.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass work is highlighted as gorgeous and central – the famed “Philo stomp” receives special praise.
band_member_comments.Bob: Rhythm guitar and vocals are noted as solid components of the well‑balanced mix, though no specific solo comments were made.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming is praised for providing a strong, steady foundation within the pristine mix.
